sanskrit_devanagari: कर्मण्येवाधिकारस्ते मा फलेषु कदाचन। मा कर्मफलहेतुर्भूर्मा ते सङ्गोऽस्त्वकर्मणि॥
transliteration: karmany evadhikaras te ma phaleshu kadachana | ma karma-phala-hetur bhur ma te sango 'stv akarmani ||
translation_english: You have a right to perform your prescribed duties, but you are not entitled to the fruits of your actions. Never consider yourself the cause of the results of your activities, and never be attached to not doing your duty.
translation_hindi: तुम्हारा अधिकार केवल कर्म करने में है, उसके फलों में कभी नहीं। कर्म के फल का कारण मत बनो और अकर्म में भी तुम्हारी आसक्ति न हो।
word_meanings: karmani=in action; eva=certainly; adhikarah=right; te=your; ma=never; phaleshu=in the fruits; kadachana=at any time; ma=never; karma-phala=result of action; hetuh=cause; bhuh=become; ma=never; te=your; sangah=attachment; astu=should be; akarmani=in not doing
purport_summary: This is the most famous verse of the Gita. Krishna instructs that one must do their duty without worrying about outcomes. Work done with attachment to results causes suffering. The yogi acts fully but remains internally free of outcome-anxiety.
modern_question_mapping: fear of failure, performance anxiety, career decisions, exam stress, business risk, creative block
speaker: Krishna
tags: karma, duty, detachment, action, fruits, anxiety, performance

sanskrit_devanagari: न जायते म्रियते वा कदाचिन्नायं भूत्वा भविता वा न भूयः। अजो नित्यः शाश्वतोऽयं पुराणो न हन्यते हन्यमाने शरीरे॥
transliteration: na jayate mriyate va kadacin nayam bhutva bhavita va na bhuyah | ajo nityah sasvato 'yam purano na hanyate hanyamane sarire ||
translation_english: The soul is never born nor dies at any time. It has not come into being, does not come into being, and will not come into being. It is unborn, eternal, ever-existing, and primeval. It is not slain when the body is slain.
translation_hindi: यह आत्मा किसी काल में भी न जन्म लेती है और न मरती है। यह न तो जन्मी है, न जन्म लेती है, न लेगी। यह अजन्मा, नित्य, शाश्वत और पुरातन है। शरीर के मारे जाने पर भी यह नहीं मारी जाती।
word_meanings: na=never; jayate=takes birth; mriyate=dies; va=either; kadacit=at any time; na=never; ayam=this; bhutva=having come into being; bhavita=will come to be; va=or; na=not; bhuyah=again; ajah=unborn; nityah=eternal; sasvatah=permanent; ayam=this; puranah=the oldest; na=never; hanyate=is killed; hanyamane=being killed; sarire=the body
purport_summary: Krishna reveals the eternal nature of the soul. The Atman is beyond birth, death, time, and physical destruction. This is the foundation of fearlessness — when you know you are the eternal soul, death loses its terror.
modern_question_mapping: fear of death, grief over loss, mortality anxiety, existential crisis, mourning a loved one
speaker: Krishna
tags: soul, immortality, death, eternal, atman, rebirth, fear of death

transliteration: matra-sparsas tu kaunteya sitosna-sukha-duhkha-dah | agamapayino 'nityas tams titikshasva bharata ||
translation_english: O son of Kunti, the non-permanent appearance of happiness and distress, and their disappearance in due course, are like the appearance and disappearance of winter and summer seasons. They arise from sense perception, O scion of Bharata, and one must learn to tolerate them without being disturbed.
translation_hindi: हे कौन्तेय! सुख-दुःख का आना-जाना शीत और ग्रीष्म ऋतुओं के आने-जाने के समान है। वे इंद्रियों के संपर्क से उत्पन्न होते हैं। हे भारत! उन्हें सहन करना सीखो।
word_meanings: matra-sparsah=sensory contacts; tu=only; kaunteya=O son of Kunti; sita=winter; usna=summer; sukha=happiness; duhkha=and pain; dah=giving; agama=appearing; apayinah=disappearing; anityah=non-permanent; tan=those; titikshasva=just tolerate; bharata=O descendant of Bharata
purport_summary: The Gita's teaching on impermanence. Just as seasons come and go, pleasure and pain are temporary visitors. The wise person observes them without being controlled by them. This is the root of emotional resilience.
modern_question_mapping: depression, emotional pain, loss, breakup, failure, grief, mood swings, resilience building
speaker: Krishna
tags: impermanence, suffering, resilience, pain, seasons, tolerance, equanimity

sanskrit_devanagari: ध्यायतो विषयान्पुंसः सङ्गस्तेषूपजायते। सङ्गात्सञ्जायते कामः कामात्क्रोधोऽभिजायते॥
transliteration: dhyayato visayan pumsah sangas tesupajayate | sangat sanjayate kamah kamat krodho 'bhijayate ||
translation_english: While contemplating the objects of the senses, a person develops attachment for them, and from such attachment lust develops, and from lust anger arises.
translation_hindi: विषयों का ध्यान करने वाले पुरुष की उनमें आसक्ति उत्पन्न होती है, आसक्ति से काम उत्पन्न होता है, काम से क्रोध उत्पन्न होता है।
word_meanings: dhyayatah=contemplating; visayan=sense objects; pumsah=of a person; sangah=attachment; tesu=to them; upajayate=develops; sangat=from attachment; sanjayate=develops; kamah=desire; kamat=from desire; krodhah=anger; abhijayate=arises
purport_summary: Krishna maps the psychological chain reaction from thought to destruction: contemplation → attachment → desire → anger → delusion → loss of memory → destruction of discrimination → ruination. This is the Gita's model of how addiction, rage, and self-destruction begin.
modern_question_mapping: anger management, addiction, obsessive thoughts, toxic relationships, social media addiction, impulse control
speaker: Krishna
tags: anger, desire, attachment, addiction, psychology, chain reaction, mind

sanskrit_devanagari: क्रोधाद्भवति सम्मोहः सम्मोहात्स्मृतिविभ्रमः। स्मृतिभ्रंशाद् बुद्धिनाशो बुद्धिनाशात्प्रणश्यति॥
transliteration: krodhad bhavati sammohah sammohat smriti-vibhramah | smriti-bhramsad buddhi-naso buddhi-nasat pranasyati ||
translation_english: From anger, complete delusion arises, and from delusion bewilderment of memory. When memory is bewildered, intelligence is lost, and when intelligence is lost one falls down again into the material pool.
translation_hindi: क्रोध से सम्मोह उत्पन्न होता है, सम्मोह से स्मृति-विभ्रम होता है, स्मृतिभ्रंश से बुद्धि का नाश होता है और बुद्धिनाश से मनुष्य का पतन हो जाता है।
word_meanings: krodhad=from anger; bhavati=comes; sammohah=delusion; sammohat=from delusion; smriti=of memory; vibhramah=confusion; smriti-bhramsad=from bewilderment of memory; buddhi-nasah=loss of intelligence; buddhi-nasat=from loss of intelligence; pranasyati=one falls down
purport_summary: Continuation of 2.62 — the psychological cascade continues from anger to total self-destruction. This is the Gita's warning about how unchecked emotion destroys the capacity for rational thought and ethical action.
modern_question_mapping: anger management, emotional regulation, rage, poor decisions under stress, addiction relapse
speaker: Krishna
tags: anger, delusion, intelligence, downfall, psychology, mind, decision-making

sanskrit_devanagari: यद्यदाचरति श्रेष्ठस्तत्तदेवेतरो जनः। स यत्प्रमाणं कुरुते लोकस्तदनुवर्तते॥
transliteration: yad yad acarati sresthah tat tad evetaro janah | sa yat pramanam kurute lokas tad anuvartate ||
translation_english: Whatever action is performed by a great man, common men follow in his footsteps. And whatever standards he sets by exemplary acts, all the world pursues.
translation_hindi: श्रेष्ठ पुरुष जो-जो आचरण करता है, दूसरे लोग भी वैसा ही करते हैं। वह जो प्रमाण (मानक) स्थापित करता है, संसार उसी का अनुसरण करता है।
word_meanings: yat yat=whatever; acarati=does; sresthah=a great man; tat tat=that; eva=certainly; itarah=common; janah=person; sah=he; yat=whichever; pramanam=standard; kurute=establishes; lokah=the world; tat=that; anuvartate=follows
purport_summary: Leaders and role models shape society. Whatever a respected person does, others imitate. This verse speaks to the immense responsibility of leadership — your example is your most powerful teaching.
modern_question_mapping: leadership, parenting, teaching, role models, social influence, setting examples, management
speaker: Krishna
tags: leadership, example, influence, role model, society, responsibility

sanskrit_devanagari: प्रकृतेः क्रियमाणानि गुणैः कर्माणि सर्वशः। अहङ्कारविमूढात्मा कर्ताहमिति मन्यते॥
transliteration: prakrteh kriyamanani gunaih karmani sarvasah | ahankara-vimudhatma kartaham iti manyate ||
translation_english: The spirit soul bewildered by the influence of false ego thinks himself the doer of activities that are in actuality carried out by the three modes of material nature.
translation_hindi: प्रकृति के गुणों द्वारा सभी कर्म किए जाते हैं। अहंकार से मोहित अज्ञानी सोचता है कि मैं कर्ता हूँ।
word_meanings: prakrteh=of material nature; kriyamanani=being done; gunaih=by the modes; karmani=activities; sarvasah=in all respects; ahankara-vimudha=bewildered by false ego; atma=the spirit soul; karta=doer; aham=I; iti=thus; manyate=thinks
purport_summary: The ego claims credit and blame for things that are driven by deeper forces of nature. This verse cuts through pride and victimhood simultaneously — you are not as much in control as you think, nor as helpless as you fear.
modern_question_mapping: ego, pride, humility, credit-taking, blame, self-importance, overconfidence, feeling controlled
speaker: Krishna
tags: ego, ahankara, false ego, doership, nature, humility, pride

sanskrit_devanagari: यदा यदा हि धर्मस्य ग्लानिर्भवति भारत। अभ्युत्थानमधर्मस्य तदात्मानं सृजाम्यहम्॥
transliteration: yada yada hi dharmasya glanir bhavati bharata | abhyutthanam adharmasya tadatmanam srjamy aham ||
translation_english: Whenever and wherever there is a decline in religious practice, O descendant of Bharata, and a predominant rise of irreligion — at that time I descend Myself.
translation_hindi: हे भारत! जब-जब धर्म की हानि और अधर्म की वृद्धि होती है, तब-तब मैं स्वयं प्रकट होता हूँ।
word_meanings: yada yada=whenever and wherever; hi=certainly; dharmasya=of religion; glanih=discrepancies; bhavati=become manifested; bharata=O descendant of Bharata; abhyutthanam=predominance; adharmasya=of irreligion; tada=at that time; atmanam=self; srjami=manifest; aham=I
purport_summary: The promise of divine intervention when evil rises. This verse establishes the cyclical nature of cosmic justice — no darkness lasts forever. The divine corrects imbalance in each age.
modern_question_mapping: despair at world events, injustice, corruption, evil rising, hopelessness about society
speaker: Krishna
tags: dharma, avatar, divine intervention, justice, evil, hope, cosmic order

sanskrit_devanagari: परित्राणाय साधूनां विनाशाय च दुष्कृताम्। धर्मसंस्थापनार्थाय सम्भवामि युगे युगे॥
transliteration: paritranaya sadhunam vinasaya ca duskrtam | dharma-samsthapanarthaya sambhavami yuge yuge ||
translation_english: To deliver the pious and to annihilate the miscreants, as well as to reestablish the principles of religion, I Myself appear millennium after millennium.
translation_hindi: साधुओं की रक्षा के लिए, दुष्टों के विनाश के लिए और धर्म की पुनः स्थापना के लिए मैं युग-युग में प्रकट होता हूँ।
word_meanings: paritranaya=for the deliverance; sadhunam=of the devotees; vinasaya=for the annihilation; ca=also; duskrtam=of the miscreants; dharma=principles of religion; samsthapana-arthaya=to reestablish; sambhavami=I do appear; yuge yuge=millennium after millennium
purport_summary: The divine protects the good and destroys evil — not once but in every age. This is the cosmic guarantee that righteousness will always be restored.
modern_question_mapping: injustice, feeling unprotected, faith in God, hopelessness, evil winning, divine protection
speaker: Krishna
tags: protection, dharma, divine, justice, avatar, yuga, faith

sanskrit_devanagari: उद्धरेदात्मनात्मानं नात्मानमवसादयेत्। आत्मैव ह्यात्मनो बन्धुरात्मैव रिपुरात्मनः॥
transliteration: uddhared atmanatmanam natmanam avasadayet | atmaiva hy atmano bandhur atmaiva ripur atmanah ||
translation_english: A man must elevate himself by his own mind, not degrade himself. The mind is the friend of the conditioned soul, and his enemy as well.
translation_hindi: मनुष्य को अपने मन द्वारा अपना उद्धार करना चाहिए, अपना पतन नहीं। मन ही मनुष्य का मित्र है और मन ही शत्रु है।
word_meanings: uddharet=one must deliver; atmana=by the mind; atmanam=the conditioned soul; na=never; atmanam=the conditioned soul; avasadayet=put into degradation; atma=mind; eva=certainly; hi=indeed; atmanah=of the conditioned soul; bandhuh=friend; atma=mind; eva=certainly; ripuh=enemy; atmanah=of the conditioned soul
purport_summary: You are your own best friend and your own worst enemy — everything depends on how you train and use your mind. Self-upliftment is not possible through external means alone; the mind must become your ally.
modern_question_mapping: self-sabotage, negative self-talk, mental health, discipline, self-improvement, inner critic, mindset
speaker: Krishna
tags: mind, self, friend, enemy, upliftment, self-improvement, mental health

sanskrit_devanagari: बन्धुरात्मात्मनस्तस्य येनात्मैवात्मना जितः। अनात्मनस्तु शत्रुत्वे वर्तेतात्मैव शत्रुवत्॥
transliteration: bandhur atmatmanas tasya yenatmaiva 'tmanā jitah | anamatnas tu satrutve vartetatmaiva satruvat ||
translation_english: For one who has conquered the mind, the Supersoul is already reached, for he has attained tranquility. To such a man happiness and distress, heat and cold, honor and dishonor are all the same.
translation_hindi: जिसने अपने मन को जीत लिया है, उसके लिए मन मित्र है। जो मन पर नियंत्रण नहीं रख पाया, उसके लिए मन ही शत्रु की तरह काम करता है।
word_meanings: bandhuh=friend; atma=the mind; atmanah=of the living entity; tasya=of him; yena=by whom; atma=mind; eva=certainly; atmana=by the living entity; jitah=conquered; anatmanah=of one who has failed to control the mind; tu=but; satrutve=in enmity; varteta=remains; atma=mind; eva=certainly; satruvat=like an enemy
purport_summary: The mind as master or slave — this verse completes the pair with 6.5. When the mind is trained, it becomes your greatest ally; untrained, it is your most destructive adversary.
modern_question_mapping: discipline, willpower, self-control, procrastination, bad habits, mental strength, meditation benefits
speaker: Krishna
tags: mind, control, discipline, willpower, enemy, friend, self-mastery

sanskrit_devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच। असंशयं महाबाहो मनो दुर्निग्रहं चलम्। अभ्यासेन तु कौन्तेय वैराग्येण च गृह्यते॥
transliteration: sri bhagavan uvaca | asamsayam maha-baho mano durnigraham calam | abhyasena tu kaunteya vairagyena ca grhyate ||
translation_english: The Blessed Lord said: O mighty-armed son of Kunti, it is undoubtedly very difficult to curb the restless mind, but it is possible by suitable practice and by detachment.
translation_hindi: श्रीभगवान बोले: हे महाबाहो! निःसंदेह मन चंचल और कठिनता से वश में होने वाला है, परंतु हे कुन्तीपुत्र! अभ्यास और वैराग्य से यह वश में होता है।
word_meanings: sri bhagavan uvaca=Lord Krishna said; asamsayam=undoubtedly; maha-baho=O mighty-armed one; manah=mind; durnigraham=difficult to curb; calam=flickering; abhyasena=by practice; tu=but; kaunteya=O son of Kunti; vairagyena=by detachment; ca=also; grhyate=can be so controlled
purport_summary: Krishna acknowledges the difficulty of controlling the mind — this is not easy and he admits it. But the solution is clear: practice (abhyasa) and detachment (vairagya). Not willpower alone, but consistent practice.
modern_question_mapping: meditation difficulty, wandering mind, giving up on habits, building discipline, ADHD focus, consistent practice
speaker: Krishna
tags: mind, practice, detachment, abhyasa, vairagya, meditation, discipline, restless

sanskrit_devanagari: अनन्याश्चिन्तयन्तो मां ये जनाः पर्युपासते। तेषां नित्याभियुक्तानां योगक्षेमं वहाम्यहम्॥
transliteration: ananyash cintayanto mam ye janah paryupasate | tesam nityabhiyuktanam yoga-ksemam vahamy aham ||
translation_english: But those who always worship Me with exclusive devotion, meditating on My transcendental form — to them I carry what they lack, and I preserve what they have.
translation_hindi: जो अनन्य भक्त मुझे निरंतर चिंतन करते हुए उपासना करते हैं, उन नित्ययुक्त भक्तों का योगक्षेम मैं स्वयं वहन करता हूँ।
word_meanings: ananyah=having no other object; cintayantah=concentrating; mam=on Me; ye=those who; janah=persons; paryupasate=properly worship; tesam=of them; nitya=always; abhiyuktanam=fixed in devotion; yoga=requirements; ksemam=protection; vahami=carry; aham=I
purport_summary: The divine promise: surrender your worries to God and your needs will be met. This is not passivity — it is the deepest trust. The devotee who acts in complete dedication to God is protected and provided for by the divine itself.
modern_question_mapping: financial anxiety, fear of the future, trusting God, surrendering control, faith in difficult times
speaker: Krishna
tags: devotion, protection, surrender, divine care, faith, bhakti, anxiety

sanskrit_devanagari: तस्मात्त्वमुत्तिष्ठ यशो लभस्व जित्वा शत्रून्भुङ्क्ष्व राज्यं समृद्धम्। मयैवैते निहताः पूर्वमेव निमित्तमात्रं भव सव्यसाचिन्॥
transliteration: tasmat tvam uttistha yaso labhasva jitva satrun bhunksva rajyam samrddham | mayaivaite nihatah purvam eva nimitta-matram bhava savyasacin ||
translation_english: Therefore get up and prepare to fight and win glory. Conquer your enemies and enjoy a flourishing kingdom. They are already put to death by My arrangement, and you can be but an instrument in the fight.
translation_hindi: इसलिए तुम उठो और यश प्राप्त करो। शत्रुओं को जीतकर समृद्ध राज्य भोगो। ये सब मेरे द्वारा पहले ही मारे जा चुके हैं। तुम केवल निमित्त मात्र बनो।
word_meanings: tasmat=therefore; tvam=you; uttistha=get up; yasah=fame; labhasva=gain; jitva=conquering; satrun=enemies; bhunksva=enjoy; rajyam=kingdom; samrddham=flourishing; maya=by Me; eva=certainly; ete=all these; nihatah=killed; purvam eva=by previous arrangement; nimitta-matram=just the cause; bhava=become; savyasacin=O Savyasaci
purport_summary: The instrument teaching — Krishna reveals that the outcome is already determined by divine will. Arjuna must act, but he is not the ultimate cause. This liberates the warrior from the burden of results while keeping him fully engaged in action.
modern_question_mapping: feeling paralyzed, over-responsibility, impostor syndrome, feeling like a fraud, taking action despite doubt
speaker: Krishna
tags: instrument, action, divine will, destiny, courage, warrior, rise up

sanskrit_devanagari: अद्वेष्टा सर्वभूतानां मैत्रः करुण एव च। निर्ममो निरहङ्कारः समदुःखसुखः क्षमी॥
transliteration: advesta sarva-bhutanam maitrah karuna eva ca | nirmamo nirahankarah sama-duhkha-sukhah ksami ||
translation_english: One who is not envious but is a kind friend to all living entities, who does not think himself a proprietor, who is free from false ego, equal in both happiness and distress, who is always satisfied and engaged in devotional service with determination — such a person is very dear to Me.
translation_hindi: जो किसी से द्वेष नहीं करता, सबका मित्र और करुणामय है, ममता और अहंकार से रहित है, सुख-दुःख में समान है और क्षमाशील है — वह मुझे प्रिय है।
word_meanings: advesta=non-envious; sarva-bhutanam=to all living entities; maitrah=friendly; karunah=kindly; eva=certainly; ca=also; nirmamah=with no sense of proprietorship; nirahankarah=without false ego; sama=equal; duhkha=in distress; sukhah=and happiness; ksami=forgiving
purport_summary: The ideal devotee described by Krishna: one who is kind to all, has no ego or possessiveness, forgives freely, and maintains equanimity. This is the Gita's blueprint for a good human being.
modern_question_mapping: how to be a good person, relationships, forgiveness, letting go of ego, compassion, jealousy, resentment
speaker: Krishna
tags: compassion, friendship, ego-free, equanimity, forgiveness, devotee, kindness

sanskrit_devanagari: सर्वधर्मान्परित्यज्य मामेकं शरणं व्रज। अहं त्वां सर्वपापेभ्यो मोक्षयिष्यामि मा शुचः॥
transliteration: sarva-dharman parityajya mam ekam saranam vraja | aham tvam sarva-papebhyo moksayisyami ma sucah ||
translation_english: Abandon all varieties of religion and just surrender unto Me. I shall deliver you from all sinful reactions. Do not fear.
translation_hindi: सभी धर्मों को त्यागकर केवल मेरी शरण में आ जाओ। मैं तुम्हें सभी पापों से मुक्त कर दूँगा, शोक मत करो।
word_meanings: sarva-dharman=all varieties of religion; parityajya=abandoning; mam=unto Me; ekam=only; saranam=for surrender; vraja=go; aham=I; tvam=you; sarva=all; papebhyah=from sinful reactions; moksayisyami=will deliver; ma=do not; sucah=worry
purport_summary: The charama shloka — the final, most essential teaching of the Gita. Surrender completely, let go of all calculations, and trust the divine absolutely. The promise is liberation from all fear and sin. This is the culmination of 18 chapters.
modern_question_mapping: complete surrender, letting go, guilt, past mistakes, redemption, religious confusion, final peace
speaker: Krishna
tags: surrender, sharanagati, liberation, fearlessness, grace, charama shloka, salvation

sanskrit_devanagari: यत्र योगेश्वरः कृष्णो यत्र पार्थो धनुर्धरः। तत्र श्रीर्विजयो भूतिर्ध्रुवा नीतिर्मतिर्मम॥
transliteration: yatra yogesvarak krsno yatra partho dhanur-dharah | tatra srir vijayo bhutir dhruva nitir matir mama ||
translation_english: Wherever there is Krishna, the master of all mystics, and wherever there is Arjuna, the supreme archer, there will certainly be opulence, victory, extraordinary power, and morality. That is my opinion.
translation_hindi: जहाँ योगेश्वर कृष्ण हैं और जहाँ धनुर्धर अर्जुन है, वहाँ श्री (समृद्धि), विजय, विभूति और अचल नीति है — यह मेरा मत है।
word_meanings: yatra=wherever; yoga-isvarah=the master of mysticism; krsnah=Lord Krishna; yatra=wherever; parthah=the son of Prtha; dhanuh-dharah=the carrier of the bow and arrow; tatra=there; srih=opulence; vijayah=victory; bhutih=exceptional power; dhruva=certain; nitih=morality; matih mama=my opinion
purport_summary: The final verse of the Gita — Sanjaya's conclusion. Where divine wisdom and devoted human effort unite, victory is guaranteed. This is the synthesis of the whole scripture: align yourself with the divine, act with full skill, and success follows.
modern_question_mapping: seeking victory, starting a business, taking on a challenge, wanting success, aligning with higher purpose
speaker: Sanjaya
tags: victory, success, divine alliance, final verse, opulence, purpose, morality
